agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific int

report.hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) binding by avoiding electrostatic complementarity in off-target pockets.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current design strategy is not effectively disrupting off-target interaction

report.hypothesis: The current design strategy is not effectively disrupting off-target interactions. A focused mutation strategy at non-pharmacophore positions is necessary to improve selectivity over native SST-14.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must target non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) where off-target contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5 are likely occurring. Mutating these positions can disrupt off-target interactions while preserving key pharmacophore residues (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).

These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the pharmacophore. For example, introducing charged or polar residues at positions 1, 2, 5, and 6 can reduce off-target binding while hydrophobic substitutions at positions 11 and 12 can modulate SSTR2-specific interactions.
